goal: Fix critical midden entries (spawn-tree O(n^2), queen-promote newline destruction, JSON ant names), reduce aether-utils.sh bug-fix ratio through targeted fixes, update 4 high-CVE dependencies (minimatch, path-to-regexp, picomatch, node-tar), and filter test noise from activity log

phases.name: Harden JSON escaping in spawn-tree
phases.description: Add missing newline/CR/control-character escaping to awk-based JSON output in spawn-tree.sh

phases.name: Fix queen-promote newline destruction and awk backslash issue
phases.description: Sanitize newlines in content before building QUEEN.md entries, fix awk -v backslash interpretation, clean up orphaned .metaupd temp files

phases.name: Filter test noise from activity log
phases.description: Add AETHER_TESTING env guard to all 5 activity.log write points in error-handler.sh and spawn.sh

phases.name: Update high-CVE dependencies
phases.description: Run npm audit fix for minimatch, path-to-regexp, picomatch, tar; add npm overrides if needed for nested picomatch

phases.name: Verify fixes and acknowledge midden entries
phases.description: Run full test suite, acknowledge 7+ resolved midden entries including already-fixed O(n^2) spawn-tree

learnings.claim: When adding awk gsub escaping, order matters: backslash must be first to prevent double-escaping of chars added by subsequent gsub calls
learnings.evidence: 13/13 spawn-tree tests pass with 5-gsub chain in correct order

learnings.claim: Literal newlines in pipe-delimited records break awk NF guards, making CR the primary testable control character scenario for field-embedded content
learnings.evidence: Builder found awk NF==7 splits on newlines, so CR is the real edge case for embedded control chars

learnings.claim: awk -v interprets C-style escapes — use ENVIRON[] instead for user content to prevent backslash destruction
learnings.evidence: 13/13 queen tests pass after replacing all awk -v with ENVIRON approach

learnings.claim: Multi-line strings cannot be passed via awk -v — use head/tail concatenation for multi-line content replacement
learnings.evidence: METADATA block update now works correctly, no more 0-byte .metaupd orphans

instincts.trigger: shell functions pass user content to awk
instincts.action: use ENVIRON[] instead of awk -v to prevent C-style backslash escape interpretation
evidence: queen.sh awk -v was destroying backslashes in content, ENVIRON fix validated by 13/13 tests

instincts.trigger: adding awk gsub escaping for JSON output
instincts.action: escape backslash FIRST to prevent double-escaping from subsequent gsub calls
When adding awk gsub escaping, order matters: backslash must be first to prevent double-escaping
Literal newlines break awk NF guards, CR is the testable control char for embedded content
awk -v interprets C-style escapes — use ENVIRON[] for user content
Multi-line strings cannot pass via awk -v — use head/tail concatenation
